reason: The rule involves modifying cryptographic sub-policies. While some sub-policies are explicitly allowed, changing crypto policies can break application compatibility, disrupt TLS handshakes, or cause service failures if the application relies on specific algorithms (like SHA1 or certain ciphers) that are being restricted. Because this affects the underlying cryptographic behavior of the system, it requires manual verification of application requirements.
estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential breakage of legacy applications relying on non-compliant algorithms; Disruption of TLS/SSL connections; Service startup failures due to unsupported crypto modules
